title: hieracium carpathicum subsp. truncatum
intro: Hieracium carpathicum subsp. truncatum, also known as the Carpathian hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infection. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Hieracium carpathicum subsp. truncatum is often used as a tincture, tea, or salve to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
